# Privacy Sandbox Demos, Pixel devices, and more dev news!

<SocialEmbed platform="youtube" id="FjZLZwOJySw" />

## Description

TL;DR 348  | The Google Developer News Show

0:00 - Introduction
0:11 - The new Pixel Fold & Pixel Tablet are here → https://goo.gle/3O2n5Be 
0:53 - Better full screen mode with the Keyboard Lock API → https://goo.gle/3PJAPCb 
1:23 - Introducing Privacy Sandbox Demos → https://goo.gle/3NDqF3x 
1:54 - Google Cloud Awards $313,337 in 2022 VRP Prizes → https://goo.gle/3rdoyvU 
2:21 - Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe! 

Here to bring you the latest developer news from across Google is Rody, a Developer Advocate. Tune in every week for a new episode, and let us know what you think of the latest announcements in the comments below.

Follow Google for Developers on Instagram → https://goo.gle/googledevs

Watch more #DevShow → https://goo.gle/GDevShow
Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers

#Google for #Developers

## Transcript

### Introduction

**0:00** · \[MUSIC PLAYING\] RODY DAVIS: Hi.

**0:05** · I'm Rody for "The Developer Show," and this is your weekly update on the coolest developer news from Google.

**0:10** · The new Pixel Fold and tablet are here.

### The new Pixel Fold & Pixel Tablet are here

**0:12** · So now, it is time to start optimizing your apps for these large and folding screens.

**0:17** · There's a lot of new tools and APIs to get started with.

**0:20** · There are three important design principles you can follow to make most of the large screens on Android.

**0:25** · There's two-pane layouts to display content across two columns, showing you more content across the unfolded, larger screen, and allows you to do more without extra taps or losing context.

**0:36** · App continuity on foldable phones ensures apps easily transition from folded to unfolded orientations.

**0:42** · Responsive layouts resize content and controls when the screen size changes, such as when you use two apps in split screen mode.

**0:49** · You can learn more on the "Android Developers Blog."

**0:52** · Next, we can head over to the web to talk about the new keyboard lock API.

### Better full screen mode with the Keyboard Lock API

**0:56** · If you've ever been playing that game and you try to close a game dialogue with the Escape key, you know how frustrating it is when it breaks the immersion and leaves the full-screen experience.

**1:05** · With the new Keyboard Lock API, available on the navigation.keyboard, you can now lock and unlock to capture all the events.

**1:12** · If the user presses the Escape key, they'll just get a dialog that says they need to press and hold Escape to exit.

**1:18** · You can learn more on the "Chrome Developers" blog.

**1:21** · Also on the web, there are new privacy sandbox demos to check out, with over 20 APIs to protect people's privacy online and to help give companies and developers tools to build thriving businesses.

### Introducing Privacy Sandbox Demos

**1:32** · All of these demos are open source and include lightweight infrastructure scripts bundled as container images.

**1:38** · You can run all those demos locally with Docker or deploy to Google Cloud.

**1:43** · There are even Google-hosted instances for everyone, including those who are just simply curious and want to start learning and experimenting.

**1:50** · You can learn more on the "Chrome Developers" blog.

**1:53** · 2022 was a successful year for the Google Vulnerability Reward Program, and they identified over 2,900 issues.

### Google Cloud Awards $313,337 in 2022 VRP Prizes

**2:00** · They awarded over $300,000 in prizes and over $12 million to bounty rewards to researchers.

**2:07** · All of these reports help improve Google Cloud products, which can help users, then customers, as well as the internet, at large.

**2:15** · You can learn more about the top seven prizes won on the "Google Security Blog."

**2:19** · To learn more about all this week's stories, make sure to check the description box below for all the links.

### Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe!

**2:24** · Please remember to like, subscribe, share, and stay safe.

**2:27** · I'm Rody for "The Developer Show."

**2:28** · Thanks for watching, and we'll see you next week.

**2:31** · All of these reports help imploo-- \[COUGHS\] help imploo.

**2:34** · One more time.

**2:35** · Yeah, thanks.
